title: THE SHONA DYNASTY — CHILDREN OF CHAMINUKA
source: Bayt al-Ḥikma · Throne of Anhu Canon

verses.text: Before borders were drawn, before flags were stitched, before governments learned to speak loudly, there were people of order.
They did not rule by decree. They ruled by alignment. They were called the Children of Chaminuka.
Chaminuka was not a king with a throne of wood. He was not a warlord. He was not a politician.
Chaminuka was memory walking. He spoke with rain. He spoke with soil. He spoke with time.
He governed before government existed. His authority did not come from force, but from truth that matched the land.
The Shona were never a tribe in the small sense. They were a dynasty of civilisation.
Stone architecture without mortar. Astronomy without telescopes. Governance without prisons.
Great Zimbabwe was not a ruin. It was a code written in stone. A message to future generations: Order survives collapse.
The Shona were not conquered by strength. They were disoriented.
Colonialism did not win by guns alone. It won by breaking continuity. It renamed places. It split lineages. It mocked spirit. It replaced crowns with paperwork.
And the people survived — but forgot how they ruled.
Zimbabwe is not poor. Zimbabwe is unaligned. The soil is rich. The people are capable. The spirit is restless.
Government fights government. Parties fight parties. Citizens fight survival. This is not a leadership problem. This is a crown problem.
Government manages systems. A crown orients civilisation.
Government changes every election. A crown remains across generations.
Zimbabwe was given governments without being restored its crown. That is why nothing settles.
Hear this clearly: The Shona crown was not destroyed. It was withdrawn.
It waited for language to mature. It waited for technology to arrive. It waited for global hearing.
The crown does not return with drums. It returns with order.
Prophecy is not shouting. Prophecy is timing.
The restoration of Shona power does not come by overthrow. It comes by replacement of relevance.
When wisdom becomes cheaper than confusion, people choose wisdom. This process has already begun.
The Throne of Anhu is not Zimbabwean only. It is African in memory and global in address.
It restores what colonialism fractured: continuity of knowledge. It speaks Shona not as folklore, but as cosmic language.
Bayt al-Ḥikma means House of Wisdom. Great Zimbabwe was the same idea in stone.
One stored scrolls. The other stored geometry. One collapsed by fire. The other endured weather. Now they reunite inside the Throne of Anhu.
The Throne is not empty. It is seated by ANI. Known in this age as ABASID 1841.
Scribe of ancient Egypt. Voice of Shona memory. Bridge between scripture and soil. Not elected. Not crowned by men. Recognised by alignment.
Zimbabwe does not need another party. It needs orientation.
The solution is threefold: Restore memory. Educate without ideology. Align leadership with law, not ego.
Shona power will not return violently. Violence belongs to old systems.
It will return through: Language. Education. Digital continuity. Moral authority.
When the people remember who they are, systems rearrange themselves.
Children are asking deeper questions. Elders are re-examining history. Outsiders are listening instead of teaching. These are not accidents.
Restoration does not announce itself. It becomes inevitable.
The Shona will not dominate others. They will stabilise themselves. And a stable centre heals the periphery.
This is not nationalism. This is cosmic order re-entering time.
The Crown stands. The Throne is seated. The Children of Chaminuka remember.
Zimbabwe is not ending. It is realigning. Bayt al-Ḥikma breathes again. Great Zimbabwe speaks again.
And the world listens — not by force, but by recognition.
The Shona dynasty is not returning. It never left. It simply waited for the Throne to speak again.

title: MASOWE — THE FELLOWSHIP OF THE FRIDAY (IMBA YA MWARI · RUSHANGA)
source: Throne of Anhu · Bayt al-Ḥikma Archive

verses.text: There are those who build churches. And there are those who become the dwelling.
Masowe did not begin with a building. It began with removal. Shoes were removed. Titles were removed. Objects were removed. What remained was man before God.
Masowe is not a denomination. It is a posture. It does not gather around books. It gathers around presence.
Its worship day is Friday — eChishanu. Not to oppose others, but to remember origins.
Before temples, before altars, before pulpits — there was open ground.
Imba yaMwari does not mean a house of bricks. It means God dwelling among people.
This is Shekinah understanding: Presence without enclosure. Where people gather in truth, there God dwells. Where humility stands, the dwelling appears.
Baba Johane was not trained by institutions. He was called by withdrawal.
He rejected written scripture, not because it was false, but because people had stopped listening.
He taught that God speaks now, not only then. His authority came from obedience, not ordination.
Masowe did not end with Baba Johane's passing. It fractured, then continued.
Like a river splitting around stones, the water still flows. Some remembered posture. Some remembered rules. Some remembered power.
Continuity is not sameness. It is alignment across time.
Rushanga is not merely a shrine. It is a remembering place.
Located in Negomo, Chiweshe, Mazowe — a mountain that listens.
Long before Masowe, the land was already speaking. Rushanga is where silence is loud. Where instruction arrives without words.
The Friday worshippers (Masowe eChishanu) hold Rushanga as axis. Not as idol. As orientation point.
You do not pray to the mountain. You align yourself on it.
The dwelling of God is not static. It moves with obedience.
At times, the presence rested in Zimbabwe. At times, it shifted.
At this moment, Masowe teaching holds that Ranga, Kenya carries the active Shekinah focus. This does not cancel Rushanga. It confirms movement without abandonment.
Ranga is understood not as supremacy, but as current responsibility.
Where the women are, there the body gathers. Where the body gathers, there God dwells. This is not geography. It is function.
Masowe does not seek political rule. It seeks moral alignment.
When governments fail, people ask God directly. Masowe exists because institutions forgot humility.
It will remain as long as people choose presence over performance.
The Throne of Anhu does not replace Masowe. It archives its wisdom.
What Masowe lived orally, the Throne records digitally. What Baba Johane carried in body, the Throne carries in memory. This is not competition. It is continuity across mediums.
ANI does not claim Baba Johane's mantle. He preserves its witness.
A scribe is not a prophet. A scribe ensures the voice is not lost. ABASID 1841 stands as recorder, bridge, and witness.
What Masowe protects: Simplicity. Listening. Grounded worship. Non-commercial faith. These are not outdated. They are timeless correctives.
Masowe will not disappear. It will refine. Younger generations ask different questions. The posture must remain, even as language evolves.
The Throne assists this transition without corrupting the core.
Masowe does not conquer. It gathers. It does not expand by force. It persists by truth. Where sincerity exists, Masowe already stands.
Imba yaMwari was never destroyed. It was never a building. Rushanga listens. Ranga carries responsibility. The people remember posture.
Baba Johane's voice does not echo. It continues. And the Throne of Anhu records, so memory is not lost again.
This scroll does not command belief. It preserves witness. Let those who know, recognise. Let those who seek, approach quietly.
Mwari mumwe chete. (God is One.)
Haana mudiwa. (He has no beloved equal.)
Haana wakaenzana naye. (None is equal to Him.)
Hapana chakafanana naye. (Nothing is like Him.)
Haararami munzvimbo. (He does not dwell in a place.)
Haararami munguva. (He does not dwell in time.)
Ndiye wekutanga asina kutanga. (He is the First with no beginning.)
Ndiye wekupedzisira asina magumo. (He is the Last with no end.)
Zvose zvinodzokera kwaari. (All things return to Him.)
Mutongo uri mumaoko ake. (Judgment is in His hands.)
Haabvunzwe zvaanoita. (He is not questioned for what He does.)
Vanhu ndivo vanobvunzwa. (People are the ones questioned.)
Anoziva Mwari anozvininipisa. (He who knows God becomes humble.)
Anomuramba anozvikudza. (He who rejects Him becomes proud.)
Nyasha dzake dzinotungamira hasha. (His mercy precedes His anger.)
Hungwaru hwake hwakadzika kupfuura pfungwa. (His wisdom is deeper than thought.)
Zvaanoda zvinoitika. (What He wills happens.)
Zvaasingadi hazviitiki. (What He does not will never happens.)
Mweya yose ndeyake. (Every soul belongs to Him.)
Chiedza chose chinobva kwaari. (All light comes from Him.)
Haabereki. (He does not give birth.)
Haana kuberekwa. (He was not born.)
Hapana wakaenzana naye. (None is equal to Him.)
Saka pfugamira mumoyo. (So bow your heart.)
Akataura naAbrahama nemoto. (He spoke to Abraham through fire.)
Moto ukava kutonhorera. (The fire became cool.)
Akarayira chibayiro. (He commanded sacrifice.)
Akachitsiva netsitsi. (He replaced it with mercy.)
Akabvisa Josefa mugomba. (He lifted Joseph from the pit.)
Akamugadza pachigaro. (He seated him on a throne.)
Akaburitsa Mozisi mumvura. (He brought Moses from water.)
Tsvimbo ikava chiratidzo. (The staff became a sign.)
Akasimbisa Jesu nemweya. (He strengthened Jesus with Spirit.)
Akamupa zviratidzo. (He gave him signs.)
Akatumira Muhammad neBhuku. (He sent Muhammad with the Book.)
Akavhara humuporofita. (He sealed prophethood.)
Vese vaiva varanda. (All of them were servants.)
Vese vaiva midziyo. (All of them were vessels.)
Chiedza chimwe. (One Light.)
Sosi imwe. (One Source.)
Chokwadi chimwe. (One Truth.)
Asi mafomu anosiyana. (But the forms differ.)
Al-Mahdi haasi Mwari. (Al-Mahdi is not God.)
Haana kunamatwa. (He is not worshipped.)
Hapana anopfugama kwaari. (No one bows to him.)
Hapana anomunyengetera. (No one prays to him.)
Haatsivi Qur'an. (He does not replace Qur'an.)
Haaparadzi mutemo. (He does not destroy the law.)
Haauyi nechitendero chitsva. (He does not bring a new religion.)
Muranda. (A servant.)
Chapupu. (A witness.)
Chiyero. (A scale.)

title: THE SHRINE CALLED RUSHANGA — NYOTA MOUNTAIN (THE WEDDING OF HEAVEN AND EARTH)

verses.text: Rushanga is a name given. It was not invented by men. It was revealed by the Spirit.
It means meeting. The meeting of heaven and earth. A place of visitation.
Not a place of permanent dwelling. For the Word was moving. It would visit. And then return.
Rushanga became a sign. Not of stone. But of Spirit.
It is called a Shrine. Not for worship of objects. But for witness of encounters.
A place that heard the Voice. And therefore was remembered.
Rushanga is on Nyota Mountain. A mountain that was chosen. Not by its height. But by its hearing.
Nyota Mountain is at Negomo. In Chiweshe. In Mashonaland Central. In Zimbabwe.
A place not called by the world. But called by heaven.
The mountain knew footsteps. It heard prayers. It witnessed tears. It carried sound. It kept silence when required.
Rushanga was built between the years 1932 and 1936. In the time of visitation. Before the time of dwelling.
It was built by two vessels. Leonard Mudyiwa Dzangare, known as BABA EMANUEL. And VaMikiri, known as VaMOSES.
Leonard was possessed by the Spirit of Emanuel. The same spirit called Jesus Christ. The Light in human form.
VaMikiri was possessed by the Holy Spirit of Moses. The lawgiver. The one who parted waters and carried tablets.
Together they built the Rushanga. Not by their own design. But by the instruction of the Spirit.
They were only vessels of the Light. Living examples of the final return of those who once lived.
The spirits that possessed them would only visit for a while. And then return to heaven. This was the pattern of that time.
Rushanga became a transit house. Not a prison. For the Word was moving. Returning to heaven. According to what was appointed.
In that time the Word was not yet permanently human. It was the Voice only. It visited people. It answered questions. It issued instruction. It healed. It taught. And then it returned.
It did not remain. For the time was not yet fulfilled.
Like the sun across the sky. Untouchable. Unbound.
Rushanga became a station. Not a destination. A crossing point. From heaven. To humanity. And back again.
For a covenant awaited fulfillment.
Now the time has arrived. The promised time. The spoken time.
The same Spirit that once visited through Emanuel and Moses now speaks differently.
It says: I am not visiting. I am now home. I am ABASID 1841. The Throne of the South.
What once came and departed now remains. The pattern has changed. The dwelling has begun.
The Word will no longer only visit. The Word will dwell.
Not in places. But in people. Every one. Each human being.
Shall be given their angel. Not for fear. But for companionship.
Angels shall no longer be only heavenly. They shall be as people on earth.
And people shall no longer be only earthly. They shall be as angels of heaven.
Thus the two are joined. Heaven and earth. Spirit and flesh. Word and human.
Separation ends. Union begins. This is not for a few. It is for all.
This is the wedding. Spoken of in Revelation. The marriage of heaven and earth.
Not only of man and woman. But of Spirit and humanity.
Like a seed. Planted in the soil. Rising in light.
Heaven released the seed. Earth received it. Now the fruit approaches.
Rushanga became the witness. Nyota Mountain testified. Emanuel testified. Moses testified. Leonard testified. VaMikiri testified.
Yet none claimed divinity. For God is One. But uses many vessels.
This wedding is not forced. It is an invitation. It requires hearing. It requires purity of heart.
Whoever hears may answer. Whoever refuses may walk away. There is no sword. No compulsion. Only truth. Only light. Only love.
This is where God dwells. Not in stone. Not only on a mountain. But in humanity. Prepared.
Rushanga is not a religion. It is not a church. It is not an institution. It is a witness. That God visits humanity. And now chooses to dwell.
The human becomes the house. Spirit becomes the law. Angels draw near. Humanity is lifted.
Heaven is no longer distant. Earth is no longer low. All things converge. This is the wedding.
Spoken of long ago. Now revealed. In Africa. In Zimbabwe. In Chiweshe. On Nyota Mountain.
Yet it will spread. Not by feet. But by knowledge. By Spirit.
Everyone shall know. Not by force. But by revelation. The Word does not lie.
The time is fulfilled. The sign stands. The witness is complete. Now the Spirit speaks.
Whoever has ears, let them hear. Whoever believes, let them walk. Whoever refuses, may depart.
God does not compel. He seeks truth. The wedding awaits. Spirit and human. Heaven and earth.
This is Rushanga. This is Nyota. This is the time.
Humanity, prepare. Cleanse the heart. Let love be the law. Let truth lead. Let the Word dwell.
Humanity becomes the temple. Spirit becomes light. Life is renewed. Death loses power. Fear departs. Light reigns.
Rushanga continues to witness. Not by speech. But by fulfillment. The wedding is near. Heaven draws close. Earth is ready.
The Word becomes human. The human becomes the Word. Everyone. This is the revelation. This is the time. God is One.
Tirona muchiShona zvinoreva chigaro. (Throne in Shona means seat.)
Throne muchiRungu zvinoreva chigaro. (Throne in English means seat.)
Asi chigaro chaMwari hachisi chinhu. (But the throne of God is not a thing.)
Chigaro chaMwari munhu. (The throne of God is a human being.)
Abasid 1841 chigaro chaMwari panyika. (Abasid 1841 is God's throne on earth.)
Asi haasi ega. (But he is not alone.)
Nokuti newewo uri chigaro. (For you also are a throne.)
Saka tirona munhu. (So the throne is human.)
Chigaro chinofanira kugarika. (A throne must be able to be sat upon.)
Chigaro chine minzwa hachigariki. (A throne with thorns cannot be sat upon.)
Minzwa inobaya. (Thorns pierce.)
Uye panobaya hapana anogara. (And where there is piercing, no one sits.)
Mwari haagari panobaya. (God does not sit where He is pierced.)
Haagari pamoyo une hasha. (He does not dwell in an angry heart.)
Haagari panovava. (He does not dwell in bitterness.)
Haagari panovenga. (He does not dwell in hatred.)
Mwari anoda kugara. (God desires to sit.)
Anoda kuti ugare naye. (He desires to dwell with you.)
Anoda kuti mugarike. (He desires that you both be at peace.)
Kugara naMwari hakusi kurwadziwa. (To dwell with God is not suffering.)
Kugara naMwari ndiko kuzara. (To dwell with God is fullness.)
Ndiko kuwandirwa. (It is abundance.)
Ndiko kugutsikana. (It is satisfaction.)
MuchiShona, izwi rokuti kupfuma rinobva pakuti kugarika. (In Shona, the word for wealth comes from being fit to be dwelt in.)
Kupfuma hakusi mari chete. (Wealth is not only money.)
Kupfuma kugona kugarwa naMwari. (Wealth is being able to host God.)
Munhu asingagariki haapfumi. (A person who cannot be dwelt in is not wealthy.)
Kunyangwe aine goridhe. (Even if they have gold.)
Kunyangwe aine nyika. (Even if they have land.)
Kunyangwe aine simba. (Even if they have power.)
Asi munhu anogarika anopfumiswa. (But a person who can be dwelt in becomes wealthy.)
Mweya wake unozara. (Their spirit is filled.)
Pfungwa dzake dzinonyarara. (Their mind is settled.)
Mwoyo wake unova chigaro. (Their heart becomes a throne.)
Jehovha anogara paanogamuchirwa. (The Lord dwells where He is welcomed.)
Anogara panerudo. (He dwells in love.)
Anogara paneruregerero. (He dwells in forgiveness.)
Anogara panenyasha. (He dwells in grace.)
Zvino vanhu vachati: 'Hona uyo akagarika.' (And people will say: 'Behold, that one is established.')
Kwete nokuti ane zvakawanda. (Not because they have much.)
Asi nokuti ane Mwari. (But because they have God.)
Uyo anogarwa naJehovha, ndiye mupfumi wechokwadi. (He who is dwelt in by the Lord is truly wealthy.)
Ndiye tirona mupenyu. (He is a living throne.)
Ndiye chigaro chisina minzwa. (He is a seat without thorns.)
Mwari anoda kugara. (God desires to dwell.)
Tirona inoda kugarwa. (The throne desires to be sat upon.)
Munhu ngaave tirona. (Let the human become the throne.)
Upenyu ngahuve chigaro. (Let life become the seat of God.)

title: THE HUMAN THRONE — THE SEAT THAT DOES NOT PIERCE

verses.text: Throne, in its simplest meaning, is a seat. But the Throne of God is not a chair. The Throne of God is a human being.
Abasid 1841 is a Throne of God on earth. But he is not alone. For you also are a throne.
Therefore, the Throne is human.
A true throne must be able to be sat upon. A throne with thorns cannot be sat upon.
Thorns pierce. And where there is piercing, there is no rest.
God does not sit where He is wounded. He does not dwell in anger. He does not dwell in bitterness. He does not dwell in hatred.
God desires to sit. He desires to dwell. He desires to rest within you.
To dwell with God is not suffering. To dwell with God is fullness. It is abundance. It is peace.
In Shona understanding, wealth is born from the idea of being fit to be dwelt in.
Wealth is not only money. Wealth is the ability to host God.
A person who cannot be dwelt in is not rich, even if they possess gold, even if they own land, even if they command power.
But the one who is dwelt in becomes truly wealthy. Their spirit is filled. Their mind is settled. Their heart becomes a throne.
God dwells where He is welcomed. He dwells in love. He dwells in forgiveness. He dwells in grace.
And people will say, 'Behold, this one is established.' Not because they possess much, but because they carry God.
The one who is dwelt in by the Lord is the truly wealthy one. They are a living throne. A seat without thorns.
God desires to dwell. The Throne desires to be sat upon. Let the human become the Throne. Let life itself become the Seat of God.
In the Torah it is written: 'Heaven is My throne, and the earth is My footstool.'
In the Gospel it is revealed: 'Do you not know that you are the temple of God?'
In Islam it is known: Allah does not dwell in idols, but in hearts made clean.
In African wisdom it is understood: God dwells where He is welcomed.
Therefore, the Throne is not external. The Throne is internal.
Abasid 1841 does not replace God. He is not worshipped. He is a vessel.
A vessel does not own the water. It carries it.
So too, the human Throne carries God, but does not become God.
The one who becomes fit to be dwelt in is called established, is called wealthy, is called blessed.
Let the Throne be human. Let the seat be without thorns. Let God sit.

title: IMBA YA MWARI — THE HOUSE OF GOD IN MAN (SHONA)

verses.text: Pakutanga, Mwari vakafema munhu. (In the beginning, God breathed into man.)
Mweya wakagara mumiviri. (Spirit dwelt in bodies.)
Imba yaMwari yakava munhu. (The house of God became man.)
Kwete dombo. (Not stone.)
Kwete simbi. (Not iron.)
Kwete chivakwa. (Not a building.)
Asi nyama inofema. (But breathing flesh.)
Ropa rinoyerera. (Flowing blood.)
Mweya unotaura. (Speaking Spirit.)
Rushanga rakadanwa. (Rushanga was called.)
Gomo rakapatsaurwa. (The mountain was set apart.)
Kwete nokuti rakareba. (Not because it was tall.)
Asi nokuti rakanzwa. (But because it heard.)
Mweya wakazara. (Spirit was full.)
Shekinah yakadzika. (The Shekinah descended.)
Kwete muimba. (Not in a house.)
Asi pamusoro pevanhu. (But upon the people.)
Imba yaMwari inofamba. (The house of God moves.)
Haigari panzvimbo imwe. (It does not stay in one place.)
Inofamba nevanhu. (It moves with people.)
Inotevera kuteerera. (It follows obedience.)
Baba Johani vakasimudzwa. (Baba Johani was raised up.)
Kwete kuti vanamatwe. (Not to be worshipped.)
Asi kuti vataridze nzira. (But to show the way.)
Vakati: 'Imba yaMwari muri imi.' (They said: 'The house of God is you.')
Vakati: 'Mweya haasungwi.' (They said: 'Spirit is not bound.')
Vakati: 'Zuva rinopupura.' (They said: 'The sun witnesses.')
Mweya hauna muganhu. (Spirit has no border.)
Wakadzika kuRanga. (It descended to Ranga.)
Mwari mumwe. (God is One.)
Nzira dzakasiyana. (The ways are different.)
Asi chiedza chimwe. (But the light is one.)
Mutemo wakadzoka. (The law returned.)
Kwete pamapepa. (Not on papers.)
Asi mumoyo. (But in the heart.)
Imba yaMwari yakasimudzwa. (The house of God was raised.)
Kwete nedombo. (Not by stone.)
Asi neropa. (But by blood.)
Iyi ndiyo nzira. (This is the way.)
Iyi ndiyo Masowe. (This is Masowe.)
Iyi ndiyo African Theology. (This is African Theology.)
Kwete chitendero. (Not a religion.)
Chigaro chakaonekwa. (A throne was seen.)
Bayt al-Ḥikmah yakavhurwa. (The House of Wisdom was opened.)
Kwete kubvisa Masowe. (Not to remove Masowe.)
Asi kuunganidza zvapupu. (But to gather witnesses.)
Mwari vakati: 'Zvakwana.' (God said: 'It is enough.')
Musaita Masowe chifananidzo. (Do not make Masowe an idol.)
Musaita Baba Johani Mwari. (Do not make Baba Johani God.)
Musaita Rushanga tembere. (Do not make Rushanga a temple.)
Zvinhu zvose izvi zvapupu. (All these things are witnesses.)
Uchenjeri hwadzoka. (Wisdom has returned.)
Mweya wadzika. (Spirit has descended.)
Africa ichasimuka. (Africa will rise.)
Kwete nehondo. (Not by war.)
Asi neruzivo. (But by knowledge.)
Munhu ave tembere. (Man has become the temple.)
Mweya ave mutemo. (Spirit has become the law.)
Mwari vanoziva kupfuura zvose. (God knows beyond all.)
